reviews.title: There is absolutely no bass, highs screeching high and nothing inbetween
reviews.text: These do not fit well and hard to keep in ear. My much cheaper earphones sound much better and much more bass. The treble is very high frequency but that is all you hear with this. Buy name brand and they would have more honest advertisement and better quality. I didn't expect to good bass for in ear earphones but this has non whatsoever and the high frequency could ruin your hearing. Maybe after few hundred hours it will sound better so will let you know. In the mean time pay a little more but get name brand.

reviews.title: Great sound, but a little bulky for an IEM
reviews.text: There are 6 drivers in here-1 DD and 5 BA, but the housings are pretty big. Definitely deep and will probably stick out of your ears, but sound great (with upgraded cable). For what you are paying, these IEMs are a steal. Sound fantastic. Find the the tips that work for you and upgrade the cable (I bought the 2.5 and 3.5 compatible cables from KBEAR and they are fantastic) and you will get as good of a sound as you will find from your source. Add a quality source (I use a Radsone EarStudio) and enjoy high quality sound on a budget.

reviews.title: Not was I was expecting
reviews.text: The reviews raved about this, but I got a different pair. There was no clip, the packaging boasted 10 hours of bluetooth. The mic didn't work for phone calls (too fuzzy). I returned these. I think their getting rid of a later edition of these headphones that aren't as good as the ones they've previously sold. You can see other reviews saying the same thing. There's no noise cancelation either. So yeah, I think they're just lying about the ones they send you.

reviews.title: These are great!
reviews.text: Great sound, and they actually fit my ears without falling out. They stay in my ears and don’t hurt even when I sleep with them on. Will buy again when these wear out!

reviews.title: ... used it I got complaints of how I sounded like I was in a
reviews.text: The first time I used it I got complaints of how I sounded like I was in a tunnel

reviews.title: These Don't last.
reviews.text:
I was pretty sad to discover that these literally lasted one road trip from Oregon to Southern California. Roughly 20hrs of drive time so I used them for music and audio books. The sound quality was okay but not great. If we had a lot of road noise I couldn't hear anything on full volume
When I tried to use them for the return trip they were even quieter and the microphone wouldn't do more then a whisper on the other end. We tried multiple phones and multiple carriers to see if that was the issue
Another issue on the 2nd round was the amount of feedback we had on the line when calling or even listening to music. I thought my phone was broken or something till I turned my Bluetooth off and just used my phone speaker. I was really excited for this but won't buy them again.

reviews.title: I do not recommend these earbuds.  They have a serious design flaw.
reviews.text:
I would have easily given these earbuds 4 or 5 stars, if it wasn't for the "low battery" warning. JLAB clearly put no thought into this. Here are my issues:

1) The first low battery warning occurs about 5 minutes before the battery dies . So if you just started your jog, or just got on the bus, you're screwed. With 6 hours of play time, the low battery notification should give you at least 20 minutes notice. Since you are almost always "out and about" when listening to headphones, it would be nice to have enough juice to finish your activity or get where you are going.

2) The low battery message (which is verbal) repeats FREQUENTLY for the brief remainder of your battery life, constantly interrupting your music. This is so annoying you wind up taking the earbuds off.

Since the earbuds are essentially useless once you get the first low battery warning, the warning serves no purpose. Giving the low battery warnings at the 30, 20 and 10 minute marks would be far less annoying and actually give you a chance to get to a charger.

reviews.title: OK Headphones
reviews.text: I think these headphones are more like 3.5 stars but I rounded up.  I have kind of a small head for an adult female, and still these headphones were too tight.  Usually I can wear headphones made for kids but not these.  These are definitely for smaller heads and small ears since the ear cups get painful after a while since they aren't big enough to fully cover my ears.  The sound is o.k., nothing to write home about, but for the price (as of this review these head phones cost $19.44) I think they are worth it.  Great for kids, not so good for adults, even if you are a pinhead.

reviews.title: High quality headphones
reviews.text: These are perfect for my 4th grade child to use at school. It is hard to find headphones with plug-ins and that aren't wireless. For my son, the ones with a plug are best to use his school issued ipad. These are sturdy and I'm hoping they last the entire school year.

reviews.title: Not really for large heads.
reviews.text: All the talk about "classroom" and "child's" in the Amazon listing really means "this is a small headset." It's not big enough for me. I have a large (7 3/4) head and this thing actually hurts my ears (presses on them too hard). If you need headphones for a child then this would work just fine. Not for me.

reviews.text: These are perfect for my 6 yr old. They fit her great and don’t get so loud that they can damage her ears.

title: 1MORE ComfoBuds Mini Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Earbuds, in-Ear Headphones with Stereo Sound, Bluetooth 5.2 Headset with 4 Mics, Clear Calls, Wireless Charging, Soothing Sound, Waterproof, White
features: True Quiet in a Small Package: We put 1MORE’s proprietary QuietMax active noise cancellation technology into the compact body of the ComfoBuds Mini to provide true quiet. With up to 40dB noise cancellation depth, you can get a more immersive listening experience. And the earbuds allow you to switch between multiple listening modes to fit different scenarios.

Smaller Size, Increased Comfort: The ComfoBuds Mini is the world’s smallest and lightest ANC earbuds. A single earbud weighs only 3.7g, and with the extra tiny size and ergonomic design, you’ll get an “invisible” but super comfortable wearing experience. Four sizes of silicone ear tips are provided to get the perfect fit for your ear and better audio performance.

Tiny Mics, Crystal-Clear Calls: Two mics hidden in the tiny body of earbud work together to filter out background noise, then accurately pick up your voice and amplify it, keeping your voice sounding clear, even when calling in a carnival parade.

Astounding Audio Performance: Tiny in size, but no compromise on sound quality. A 7mm dynamic driver produces a rich, powerful, authentic sound that is usually reserved for big bulky headphones. And the integration of SoundID technology allows you to get your perfect sound that fits your ears and preference.
Convenient Wireless Charging: The ComfoBuds Mini TWS earbuds can power up super easily on the go with Qi-compatible wireless charging, say goodbye to tangled cables.

reviews.title: Mini, but Mighty
reviews.text:
Look, we know you aren't here for premium sound quality if you're looking at the tiniest earbuds and in this price range. That being said, these do still sound quite nice! There's a lot of oomph for such a small package. Unless you're used to professional grade sound equipment, these will cover most anyone's sound needs.

SMALL EAR HAVERS! I have been on a quest to find comfy earbuds. So far, these have come the closest. I can wear them for like 2 hours with minimal discomfort. This is a huge upgrade from "most cause physical pain near immediately". When placed correctly, they're pretty solid. I couldn't shake them out. They did come loose a handful of times and needed to be readjusted, but in comparison to the others needing that every 5 minutes? I'll take it.

They say they have ANC, but honestly, I'm not so sure. In my personal experience, turning ANC on didn't actually cancel any noise. It just brought the bass out. Unless I just can't get the right fit, the ANC mode is effectively just a "make the music sound better" button.

The drawback here is that you can only use ANC if both buds are in. Not usually a problem, unless you want to pop one in to listen to music and leave the other ear open for communication. The make the music sound better button won't do anything with just one bud.

reviews.title: Basically perfect, fantastic wind ANC, perfect case
reviews.text:
I needed a replacement for my Galaxy buds pro's that have the fatal ANC "roar in one ear" that Samsung won't fix, and they don't stay in the ear. Bring an absolutely ENORMOUS fan of the 1more comfobuds z (the single best sleep focused earbuds at any price) I liked that these are also small, the case shape is wonderful (flip top, tiny, wireless charger) and they're relatively inexpensive.

The sound quality uses 1more's app to do a "sounds" hearing preference test. I would prefer a proper EQ option but it works well enough. I prefer real audiophile iem's and headphones, but enjoyed the Galaxy buds on bass boost, they almost hit like planar magnetics. These are about 80% there. They're totally serviceable, with the only standout being a pretty good technical performance, with genuinely solid imaging and separation. They absolutely do need the soundID set up though, I hate the stock tuning. For tiny little things they are, I am perfectly happy with the sound quality when I want music on my bike or public transit.

The ANC is stellar. They have a specific mode for wind reduction that downright curbstomps Samsung and apple. If you ride a bike, you need 1more ANC buds. The transparency mode is great too, very natural sounding. It doesn't have that "talk and it switches to transparency" gimmick, but I found it obnoxious on Samsung.

Touch controls are easily customized and no "single touch" feature means you don't accidentally press anything readjusting or putting them in. Auto pauses when you take one out, your can use just one or the other no problem

The app features depend on the product but these are meant to be usable for sleeping, do they come preloaded with a library of sleep/ambient sounds. I just use my own but it's a nice feature. I can easily fall asleep with them in and I'm a very picky side sleeper, but I do prefer the absolutely microscopic comfobuds z's.

The stock eartips did bother me a bit, they have a little ridge from the mold that irritated my ears after a few hours of use. They have a very odd mounting system that I was only able to find one option for, spinfit 1025 tips are shallow enough to fit and still let the case close (the wells for the eartips is a bit to shallow, id like it if they addressed this on a revision) so if you don't like the tips, spinfit 1025's.

The case magnets that hold the buds in are hilariously strong. Your have to SMACK it to dislodge them. Very nice detail. The case size is tiny and perfectly shaped to slide into pockets, with a flip top that you can open one handed

For being so small they also stay put well. Not perfect, but I can work out or mow the lawn, an impossible task of the Galaxy buds pro.

Overall though, these things rock. They're exactly what I need as a paramedic who also rides a bicycle. 1more is a serious brand with some seriously well thought out products at extremely competitive prices. I can sleep on my side with a podcast and still be able to wake up for a call, they crush wind nose when I'm riding my bike, they sound perfectly adequately tuned with impressive imaging (once you run the sound id feature once) and the case is (slightly too shallow recessions for the eartips aside) very nice in the hand and perfectly shaped.

Highly recommend.

reviews.title: Bought this twice- love it
reviews.text:
I bought a pair of these about 4 ish months ago and they’re great - a terrific value for the price point.

2 weeks ago the earbuds disappeared into the ether (thanks cats) and they needed to be replaced-I explored getting something different but the Comfobuds minis check all the boxes.

The only drawback is you have to download the app to your phone and your earbuds need to be paired to your phone in order for you to check the power levels of the earbuds and the charging case.

reviews.title: Works great.
reviews.text: It's attractive enough, stays in place, and looks like it will last. There really isn't a lot to add.

reviews.title: For my son’s gaming headset...
reviews.text: This little contraption is quite awesome. Bought it for the headset my son uses while playing video games. Was tired of finding his headset left on the floor, so attached this to his entertainment center and now he hangs his $100 headset on this (up off the floor where it won’t get stepped on/ damaged).  Solid construction. Easy install (no holes, no tools/ other hardware required).

reviews.title: Great purchase
reviews.text: It was just a tad bit too small for where I wanted it,  but I found the perfect spot on the printer stand next to the desk. Very well made.  Not just a cheap piece of plastic.  Strong and sturdy. Folds up out of the way when not in use.

reviews.title: This Gadget is Great!
reviews.text: Love this thing! Put it under my desk to hold my purse. How easy can it be?! Slide it on. Tighten it securely. Add purse. Done and done! Nice quality, too…not cheesy or flimsy.

reviews.title: Great and well made
reviews.text: This thing is great and well made from metal.  This is my second one.  It cost more than the plastics ones, but it is very well made.  The holder folds up once you have it clamped to a desk to shelf.  It is strong and supports heavy headphones well.  It is a little expensive but well worth the investment.

reviews.title: Great for outside work
reviews.text:
Was wearing these when taking down Christmas decorations and listening to an audio book.

My neighbor thought it was ignoring him but I just didn’t hear him - these have good noise cancellation. Sound quality was great too.

reviews.title: Solid Value
reviews.text: Regular ear buds don't fit in my ears so I need the "wings." I am very happy with how they perform but they are not as "full range" as more expensive brands.

reviews.title: Good customer service
reviews.text: I had some issues with my first set and the customer service was awesome. I appreciate a company that will backup their products.

reviews.title: Love these
reviews.text: These are comfortable and easy to use. Good sound as well.

reviews.title: Good sound quality
reviews.text:
These are good quality but my left ear is uncomfortable. My ear must be a different shape. I was hoping the battery life would be better, but its ok. I use it to listen to books while i work. Take calls when needed.
Most bluetooth headsets have a delay or echo. These do not. It makes phone calls very clear.
These are the best sound quality of all but my Dewalt headsets.
The battery lasts about 5 to 6 hours with my streaming usage. Probably last much longer with just calls.
My left ear hurts after a couple hours, but ny right ear is fine for all day.
They charge quickly about an hour maybe a little more.
DON'T MISPLACE THE CHARGING CASE they will become worthless. They can't be charged without it.
And it seems if they power off by themselves they have to be reset in the case.
All that being said i probably would buy them again.

The pattern on the case is attractive and makes sure you don't see fingerprints. This is a must have for attractiveness and safety .

reviews.title: Great earbuds that fit well, sound good, and have long battery life!
reviews.text: The earbuds have pretty good sound and the main reason I love them is most earbuds don't fit well in my ears, but the ear fins make it snug and a good fit. They are fairly easy to connect to bluetooth devices. The case seems to hold a lot of battery and lasts a long time and I like that it charges with USB-C. The ear fins on the earbuds make it very snug and a good fit in my ears, and this also makes it noise cancelling. The case is a nice small size too. I've used it for almost a month and I have no complaints about them :)

reviews.title: I'm not keen on the cover material
reviews.text:
While the Arae Electronic Organizer does what it says, its implementation isn't great.

The double layers of this product has one and a half flap of elastic bands to hold bundled up wires, that's 18 spots total. The rest are two large pockets and a small pocket. That's plenty of room to carry most charging accessories that you'd have with you if you were equipped with tablets and phones. For laptops and cameras, you'll need other solutions. Even action camera chargers may have a hard time fitting in this slim product.

It's not a problem that this thing is pretty much made out of nylon from the outside in but the material that makes up the exterior of this product makes it look cheap. It's a rough, synthetic material that just makes it a bit cheap looking. I think cloth or leather would have been a better choice.

In addition, the zipper sticks, making it hard to quickly open this up to retrieve your wires for charging.

reviews.title: Case closed
reviews.text:
It's pretty amazing how much this will hold and still close up properly. I am using it for many cables and some adapters, and I am also keeping those little instruction manuals that come with everything in one of the zipper pouches. You can keep a lot of cables and adapters in this, and it will still zip up and be small enough for travel.
I like this case a lot.

reviews.title: Smaller than expected
reviews.text: Water proof? Not going to try that. This is super slim so you can't really fit a whole lot in. Wires, maybe... but devices, like portable routers, not exactly. Slim portable chargers, sure. Higher capacity batteries, nope. Won't fit anything more than two iPhones thick.

reviews.title: No More Ziplock Bags
reviews.text: This is such a better alternative to tossing cables into a ziplock bag.  The nice bag with zipper allows me to keep everything in one place.  I wished the compartments were a little wider, but it's easy to just use two compartments. Nice quality bag, once I get spares I would just pack these with a spare ready and be ready to go anytime.  Nice organization addition to my travel gear.

reviews.title: Handy little case
reviews.text: Does a good job at cutting down on cable clutter. I took a black Sharpie and identified the various cords by writing abbreviations on the cloth stretch band that secures the cords. It also helps organize miscellaneous adapters and charging units. Not sure how long it will last but for organizing cords and small electronic items it’s a good buy.

description:
⭕[SUPPORT PD & QC FAST CHARGING]
This is an upgraded version that supports PD and QC charging protocols. Maximum power is 9V*2A=18W.

Built-in Realtek advanced digital audio processing chip, 32-bit stereo audio DAC, 384K sampling rate, active noise reduction, gives you Hi-res sound performance. Conversion distortion is less than 1%. You can use a not particularly bad earbuds for a simple comparison test.
⭕[CLEAR AUDIO MUSIC & CALLING]
High Resolution Audio by DAC is pure without any unpleasant bottom noise. With a higher dynamic range and 120dB Signal Noise Ratio, it can ensure good resolution in both low-frequency and high-frequency, and accurately reproduce all details in music. Good high frequency extension performance. The medium frequency is clean and full, with flexibility and strength, the voice is friendly and natural. The low frequency is thick and heavy, giving you a powerful pleasure.
⭕[COMPATIBLE WITH]
This 2-in-1 USB C to 3.5mm Aux Headphone Charging Adapter is specailly designed for USB-C phones without 3.5mm audio port, you can listen to music with 3.5mm earbuds and fast charging at the same time. Support remote control and low noise calls. It's a little tight for iPad Pro.

features: 💓[HUMANIZED UPGRADE] You can connect the phone, headphones and charge cable together in any order, You do not have to connect in order.

🚀[HIGH FIDELITY OUTPUT]High-Excursion drivers that allow for greater dynamic range, improved bass, and lower distortion. Signal Noise Ratio (SNR) 120dB. Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) 90dB. Professional class G headphone output. Active noise cancellation (ANC) feature. Support 44.1k-384k/32bit full frequency sampling. So many excellent parameters ensure an immersive & vivid gaming & music experience.

🌱[HI-RES SOUND] The Hi-Res certified DAC converter provides high-resolution audio, which can express the ultimate quality of music and the reproduction of the original sound, and get the real feeling of the original singer or performer in the live performance. Very suitable for HIFI earbuds. Because the chip is ultra-low power, compared to those with high power consumption, it can effectively extend the phone battery life without worrying about battery overload.

🚩[KEY UPDATES & FAST CHARGING] [1]-Support PD & QC Fast Charing. [2]-Seamless USB C male connector, ensure better anti-interference transmission. Support remote control and low noise calls. (Volume +/- of original Apple 3.5mm earpods is not supported.)

⭕[COMPATIBLE WITH] The following device are recommended: For iPad Pro (2020, 2018) 12.9 & 11 & iPad Air 2020; For Samsung Galaxy S21 S20(Ultra, FE 5G), Note 20 10(Ultra), Z Filp, Z Fold 2, A80, Tab S5e/S6; For Google Pixel 2/2XL/3/3XL/3A/3A XL/4/4XL/5/6/6 Pro, Essential Phone. description:
Matekxy AirPods Pro 2nd silicone case is designed for the new 2022 AirPods Pro 2nd only.
The AirPods Pro 2nd Case Cover is a one-piece design, with the top cover connected to the cover.
It fits tightly with the AirPods Pro 2nd and will not come off as easily.
After putting the protective cover on the headset, the LED light is visible so you can know if the case is charging or if it is fully charged.
There is also a small flag at the bottom to be able to cover the charging port. Covering the small flag when not charging will effectively prevent dust from entering.
In addition, you can hang it outside your backpack or on your hand with the configured keychain and lanyard, very convenient to carry.
What you'll get:
AirPods Pro 2nd Silicone Case

·Surface coated with hydrophobic materials, not easy to dust, convenient washing.

features: ★【NOTE】★: AirPods and AirPods charging station are not included.

reviews.title: Nice cable
reviews.text:
Pros:
Not to expensive
Very nice looking cable
Well put together
Very supple

Cons:
None at the moment, will update if something comes up

Other thoughts
I am using Hifiman Ananda' with this cable hooked up to a Schiit Jotunheim, hooked up to a MHDT Istanbul, hooked up via USB from my monster manchine running Windows 11
It sounds lushes kind of like a full figured woman

reviews.title: Youkamoo
reviews.text: Youkamoo must translate to extreme quality for pocket change .

reviews.title: Just pull the trigger you will thank me later
reviews.text:
Wow! I own hifiman arya headphones attached to topping d90 DAC & a90 amp.
I replace the default cable with this in the sound Improvement is incredible! It may have to do with the fact that it is an XLR connection as opposed to the quarter inch connection....

Nonetheless this has made these headphones sound even more incredible

reviews.title: Sounds, looks & feels very nice
reviews.text: It's just what you'd hope, might look a little better in person. Sound is excellent. Connections are just right.

reviews.title: Sweeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeet!
reviews.text:
These headphone cables just flat out look sweeeet! I bought them for their looks alone and to be honest, they do NOT make any sonic difference in my music and they should NOT! That sort of drivel is the rote of the dreaded frAudiophile. I listen to music, not equipment. But I do look at my equipment and these cables look great!

Buy them if you want to class up your 'phones. I did!

Trust me. I know what I'm doin' when I'm reviewin'.
